cxu_object.claim: A V-dip entry is triggered when a 5-minute candle closes with a body drop of eight dollars or more followed by the next candle closing green indicating a bounce has started and the auto-buyer should market buy one ETH per dip and pyramid into subsequent dips up to a hard ETH cap without moving the safety stop loss on adds

supporting_contexts.text: Pattern validated over 10 days of live trading on ETH-USD. The V-dip appears as a single large red 5m candle (body < -$8) followed by a green recovery candle. Entry at the recovery close with SL at the dip low minus $3 buffer. The pattern bounces 70%+ of the time on 5m timeframe based on observed trades.

Pyramiding rules: add 1 ETH per V-dip up to a hard cap (currently 15 ETH). Do NOT move the safety SL when adding — the wide SL stays in place to give room for subsequent dips. No cooldown between adds — the ETH cap is the only limit. When flat and a V-dip occurs, auto-buy and place initial SL. When already long and under cap, auto-add without touching SL.

Critical lessons learned: (1) Restart of dip buyer can re-trigger on the same dip — persist last_dip_candle_t to disk. (2) Do not use portfolio percentage as pyramid cap with leveraged positions — use simple ETH cap instead. (3) The dip buyer should not place tight SLs that fight with the wider safety SL.

dipThreshold.description: Minimum 5m candle body drop in dollars to trigger

maxEthCap.description: Maximum total ETH position from dip buys

addSizeEth.description: ETH to add per dip trigger

slBufferUsd.description: SL placed this many dollars below the dip low on initial entry
